title: Veo 3.1 Lite Released
summary: Veo 3.1 Lite is a cost-effective video generation model now available in paid preview. It can be accessed through the Gemini API and tested in Google AI Studio.
body:
Veo 3.1 Lite, the latest iteration of Google's video generation model, has been released in paid preview. This model is designed to be more cost-effective than its predecessors, making it accessible to a wider range of developers and businesses. The model can be integrated into applications using the Gemini API, allowing for seamless video generation capabilities.

 The release of Veo 3.1 Lite marks a significant step forward in video generation technology. By making this model available in paid preview, Google is enabling developers to test and refine their applications, driving innovation in the field. The model's cost-effectiveness is also likely to democratize access to video generation capabilities, allowing smaller businesses and individuals to leverage this technology.

 As Veo 3.1 Lite becomes more widely adopted, it will be interesting to see the various applications and use cases that emerge. The model's availability in Google AI Studio will also provide developers with a sandbox environment to test and experiment with the technology, potentially leading to new breakthroughs and discoveries.
twitterThread: 🚀 Introducing Veo 3.1 Lite, the most cost-effective video generation model yet! 📹
Here's why this matters: Veo 3.1 Lite is now available in paid preview through the Gemini API, making it accessible to more developers and businesses.
The wild part: this model can generate high-quality videos at a fraction of the cost of its predecessors. Let that sink in.
This changes everything for small businesses and individuals who want to leverage video generation technology without breaking the bank.
What's next? We can expect to see a surge in innovative applications and use cases as Veo 3.1 Lite becomes more widely adopted.
standaloneTweet: Veo 3.1 Lite is revolutionizing video generation with its cost-effective model, now available in paid preview through the Gemini API! https://ainformed.dev/articles/veo-3-1-lite-released
